summary: The plan passes plan-approve and the per-bead complete gates HONESTLY, no overrides needed. (1) ADR-0032 exclusion is CORRECT: its current Domain(s)=[validation,adr,lifecycle] (verified in ADR-0032-adr-semantic-gates.md L5) do not intersect the impacted `workflow` domain, so citing it would trip checkADRCitations adr-cite-irrelevant (plan.go L479-485: empty intersectFold over impactedDomains=[workflow] -> AddError). Excluding-but-amending is the right call; the amendment + Domain(s) edit are authored in Bead 1 and documented in ## ADR Fitness, satisfying the adr-fitness-missing check (a Draft plan needs an ## ADR Fitness section, present at L28). (2) Coverage OK: ADR-0036 (Accepted, Domain(s) workflow,validation,doc-sync,ownership) and ADR-0024 (Accepted, Domain(s) adr,context-system,workflow) both declare `workflow` and are Accepted -> checkADRCoverage finds coveredAccepted -> silent pass, adr-coverage-missing will NOT fire; checkADRCitations finds non-empty intersection for both -> no adr-cite-irrelevant. (3) THE SUBTLE ONE resolves clean: the ADR-0032 doc edit lands at .mindspec/docs/adr/ADR-0032-adr-semantic-gates.md -> isDocFile (docsync.go L332 .mindspec/docs/ prefix) -> isProcessArtifact (divergence.go L261) -> skipped BEFORE attribution (divergence.go L183), so it cannot trip adr-divergence-unowned and needs no OWNERSHIP claim. No self-referential trap: candidateDomains for spec 100's OWN divergence gate is built from meta.Domains=[workflow] (a bare NAME), NOT from ADR-0032's Domain(s); adding `workflow` to ADR-0032 mid-spec only affects whether a FUTURE plan may cite ADR-0032 for workflow -- it does not change spec 100's own beads' citations (which cite 0036/0024, already covering). The gate reads ADR Domains only to test citation relevance/coverage of the CITED set, and ADR-0032 is not cited. (4) Every changed source file (internal/validate/**, internal/approve/**, internal/adr/**, cmd/**) is claimed by the workflow OWNERSHIP.yaml paths: (verified), workflow is covered by cited Accepted ADR-0036/0024 -> each bead's complete-time divergence gate passes, no --override-adr. (5) NO self-block on the unfixed binary: the spec declares `workflow` by NAME (ParseSpec, spec.go L66-82, takes pre-colon text -> `workflow`, a real domain dir), so the CURRENT name-equality resolution in divergence (LoadOwnership dir lookup) and the plan-time gates already resolve it correctly; nothing in the plan's gate-passing depends on the not-yet-implemented normalize-at-source helper. The plan's `mindspec validate spec`=0-errors claim holds on the running binary. One non-blocking observation: a lifecycle-binding WARN may appear pre-spec-approve (already acknowledged in spec Validation Proofs); WARNs do not block. APPROVE.
